title_en: 46. And Laqeet ibn Sabarah said: Rasoolullah (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa salam) said
title_ar: الحديث السادس والأربعون: رواية لقيط بن صبرة رضي الله عنه
content_en: Complete the wudoo and wipe between the Asaabi' (fingers and/or toes). [Abu Dawood (no.142), at-Tirmidhee (no.37), an-Nasaa'ee (no.114), Ibn Maajah (no.407), al-Hakim (1/148). Al-Albaanee says: sahih.]. As-San'aanee says: It clearly means both the fingers and toes and is shown clearly in the hadith of Ibn 'Abbaas. [Subul-us-Salaam, 1/47]. THE REPLY TO THOSE WHO FOLLOW WIPING OF THE FEET WITHOUT WASHING (1) Wiping the feet when not wearing anything on the foot is not established from the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam). As for their using as a proof the reading of the Aayah: wamsahoo bi ru'oosikum wa arjulakum ilaal ka'bayn: rub your heads and your feet to the ankles...[soorat-ul-Maaidah 5:7] reading (arjulikum) - and saying that the feet are thus connected to (the command to wipe) the heads - and this is not correct, rather they are connected to (the order to wash) the hands. As for the reading of the word (arjulikum) with Kasra of the laam - as-San'aanee says That is for wiping over the leather socks - as is shown by the Sunnah - and this is the best interpretation for this particular recital. (As-Subul, 1/58). (2) The Qur'aan is not to be explained according to the human intellect - especially in the matters of worship such as Salaat and wudoo , etc. - but by the Sunnah which explains this Pillar. And there are many such examples in the Quran which the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) explained by his sayings - and from this is the washing of the feet - and its proof from the Sunnah has preceded. (3) If they wish to use the intellect then we say to them: The bottom of the foot has more right to be wiped than the surface (their saying being the wiping of the surface), and if they say: Then what about the socks? we say: That is established from the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) just as washing the foot is also established in the Sunnah. (4) Leaving out one of two verbs and sufficing with one of them, as the Arabs, when two verbs come together having similar meaning and are followed by things related info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com to them, makes it permissible to mention only one of the two verbs and attaching those things relating to the second to those relating to the first which is mentioned - according to what the wording demands, until it is as if the two are partners with regard to the verb, as the poet said: I fed it with hay and cold water. And what is meant is: I fed it with hay and gave it cold water to drink. (5) The saying of az-Zajjaj (a great scholar of the language): It is permissible that the wording (arjulikum) has the meaning of 'wash the feet' as the wording (ilal Ka'bain) conveys that meaning - as the mentioning of a limit suggests washing just as Allaah ta'ala says ilaal maraafiq [that is the limit of the elbows is for washing] however, if wiping were intended then there would be no need for mention of a limit just as Allaah ta'ala says wamsahoo bi ru'oosikum not mentioning any limit (for wiping the head) and further the term wiping can be used to mean washing. (AlMirqaat, 1/400). (6) And further, the great majority of scholars have agreed that it is obligatory to wash the feet and that is reported - and reaches the level of mutawaatir from the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) as al-Haafiz Ibn Hajr says; and further it is not established that any of the Sahaabah differed regarding that - except what is reported from 'Alee, Ibn 'Abbas and Anas (radhiAllaahu 'anhum) - and it is established that they went back on that. (Al-Mirqaat. 1/400~.
title_id: Hadis 46: Jawaban atas Pendapat Mengusap Kaki Tanpa Membasuh
content_id: Laqit ibn Sabarah berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Sempurnakanlah wudu dan ratakanlah air di antara asabi (jari-jari tangan dan kaki). [Abu Dawud (no. 142), At-Tirmizi (no. 37), An-Nasai (no. 114), Ibnu Majah (no. 407), Al-Hakim (1/148). Al-Albani berkata: Sahih]. As-Sanani berkata: Hadis ini jelas mencakup jari tangan maupun jari kaki, dan hal ini ditunjukkan secara gamblang dalam hadis Ibnu Abbas. [Subul as-Salam, 1/47]. JAWABAN BAGI YANG MENGIKUTI PENDAPAT MENGUSAP KAKI TANPA MEMBASUH. (1) Mengusap kaki tanpa memakai alas kaki tidak pernah ditetapkan dari Nabi ﷺ. Adapun dalil mereka menggunakan bacaan ayat: wamsahu bi rususikum wa arjulikum ilal kabayn yang dibaca (arjulikum) dengan kasrah pada lam, maka As-Sanani berkata bahwa itu untuk mengusap kaus kaki dari kulit sebagaimana yang ditunjukkan oleh Sunnah. Itulah tafsir terbaik untuk bacaan tersebut. (2) Al-Quran tidak boleh ditafsirkan berdasarkan akal manusia, terutama dalam masalah ibadah seperti salat dan wudu, melainkan dengan Sunnah yang menjelaskannya. (3) Mayoritas ulama telah sepakat bahwa membasuh kaki adalah wajib, dan hal itu diriwayatkan secara mutawatir dari Nabi ﷺ sebagaimana dikatakan Al-Hafiz Ibnu Hajar. Tidak ada satu pun Sahabat yang berbeda pendapat tentang hal itu, kecuali yang dilaporkan dari Ali, Ibnu Abbas, dan Anas (radhiyallahu anhum), dan telah tetap bahwa mereka mencabut pendapat tersebut. [Al-Mirqat, 1/400].
content_ar: Complete the الوضوء and wipe between the Asaabi' (fingers and/or toes). [أبو داود (no.142), at-Tirmidhee (no.37), an-Nasaa'ee (no.114), Ibn Maajah (no.407), al-Hakim (1/148). Al-Albaanee says: صحيح.]. As-San'aanee says: It clearly means both the fingers and toes and is shown clearly in the الحديث of ابن عباس. [Subul-us-Salaam, 1/47]. THE REPLY TO THOSE WHO FOLLOW WIPING OF THE FEET WITHOUT WASHING (1) Wiping the feet عندما not wearing anything on the foot is not established from النبي ﷺ (ﷺ). أما their using as a proof the reading of the Aayah: wamsahoo bi ru'oosikum wa arjulakum ilaal ka'bayn: rub your heads and your feet to the ankles...[soorat-ul-Maaidah 5:7] reading (arjulikum) - and saying that the feet are وهكذا connected to (the command to wipe) the heads - and this is not correct, rather they are connected to (the order to wash) the hands. أما the reading of the word (arjulikum) with Kasra of the laam - as-San'aanee says That is for wiping over the leather socks - as is shown by the السنة - and this is the best interpretation for this particular recital. (As-Subul, 1/58). (2) The Qur'aan is not to be explained وفقاً لـ the human العقل - ولا سيما in the matters of worship مثل Salaat and الوضوء , etc. - but by the السنة which explains this Pillar. And there are many such examples in the القرآن الكريم which النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) explained by his sayings - and from this is the washing of the feet - and its proof from the السنة has preceded. (3) إذا they wish to use the العقل then we say to them: The bottom of the foot has more الحق to be wiped than the surface (their saying being the wiping of the surface), and إذا they say: Then what about the socks? we say: That is established from النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) just as washing the foot is أيضاً established in the السنة. (4) Leaving out one of two verbs and sufficing with one of them, as the Arabs, عندما two verbs come together having similar meaning and are followed by things related info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com to them, makes it permissible to mention only one of the two verbs and attaching those things relating to the ثانياً to those relating to the أولاً which is mentioned - وفقاً لـ what the wording demands, حتى it is as إذا the two are partners فيما يتعلق بـ the verb, as the poet said: I fed it with hay and cold water. And what is meant is: I fed it with hay and gave it cold water to drink. (5) The saying of az-Zajjaj (a great scholar of the language): يجوز that the wording (arjulikum) has the meaning of 'wash the feet' as the wording (ilal Ka'bain) conveys that meaning - as the mentioning of a limit suggests washing just as Allaah ta'ala says ilaal maraafiq [that is the limit of the elbows is for washing] غير أن, إذا wiping were intended then there would be no need for mention of a limit just as Allaah ta'ala says wamsahoo bi ru'oosikum not mentioning any limit (for wiping the head) and further the term wiping can be used to mean washing. (AlMirqaat, 1/400). (6) And further, the great majority of العلماء have agreed that يجب to wash the feet and that is reported - and reaches the level of mutawaatir from النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) as al-Haafiz Ibn Hajr says; and further it is not established that any of the Sahaabah differed فيما يخص that - except what is reported from 'Alee, ابن عباس and أنس (رضي الله عنهم) - and ثبت that they went back on that. (Al-Mirqaat. 1/400~.
